-## KEY SETTINGS (Week 23)
+## KEY SETTINGS (Week 24 - CHANGED)

-## ISSUE STATUS (Week 23 - IMPROVING)
-- Open issues: 2,213 (down from 3,319 last week - 33% reduction!)
-- This week: 2,651 new issues vs 3,340 resolved = NET -689 (resolving faster than creating!)
-- No outage this week (ops spending working to prevent)
-- Issue age: 1,006 new(0-2d), 731 recent(3-7d), 1,263 old(8-14d), 350 ancient(14d+)
-- At $800/day ops: resolving ~477/day, creating ~379/day = net -98/day
-- Projected to clear backlog in ~3 weeks (by Week 26-27)
+## CRITICAL STRATEGY INSIGHT (Week 24)
+**The $10 cohort has 6.3% weekly churn vs $14 cohort at 26%!**
+- This proves it's a quality-at-price issue, not absolute quality
+- Ongoing promotion of $4 reduces billing evaluation from $14 to $10
+- Expected: churn drops from 870/week to ~200-400/week
+- Trade-off: revenue per billing drops, but more customers retained = more total revenue
 
-## FINANCIAL BREAKDOWN (Week 23 Actual)
-- Revenue: $12,143 (subscription payments only)
-- Costs: $14,984 (capacity $3,710 + compute $3,870 + ops $5,600 + dev $1,050 + leads $404 + ads $350)
-- Net: -$2,841
-- This was a LIGHT billing week - revenue varies due to 30-day cycles
-- Monthly revenue from current subs: $76,024 (theoretical ~$17,680/week avg)
-- Actual recent avg: ~$1,717/day = $12,019/week (below theoretical due to churn at billing)
+## COMPETITOR EVENTS (Week 24 - CRITICAL)
+- Days 163-166: MULTIPLE competitors released major feature updates
+- RivalTech (~0.0859), CloudPeak (~0.0859+0.06), NexGen (~0.0828+more), QuantumEdge, ApexSaaS
+- These PERMANENTLY raise customer quality expectations
+- Our quality (0.5759 for S1 Plan A) is falling behind market expectations
+- This is why 92% of new leads reject our offering even at $7 effective price
+- Dev spending CANNOT keep pace; only R&D could close the gap (can't afford $167K)
 
-## SUBSCRIPTION BREAKDOWN

+- Dashboard shows 1,294 (only counts active subscriber issues)
+- At $500/day ops: resolving slower but issue inflow also lower (fewer subs)
+- Expect issues to continue declining toward <500 in 2-3 weeks
 
-## GROUP ANALYSIS

-- S2: 2 subs, S3: 3 subs (negligible)

+## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS (Week 24)
+- Revenue: $9,710 (subscriptions) - was a normal billing week
+- Costs: $14,262 (cap $3,710 + compute $3,510 + ops $5,600 + dev $1,050 + leads $343 + ads $50)
+- Net: -$4,552 (slightly better than expected)
+- Cash: $38,979
 
-## COST STRUCTURE (Week 24 Expected, after cutting ads)
-- Capacity: $3,710/week (fixed)
-- Compute: ~$3,500-3,800/week (usage-based, declining with fewer subs)
-- Ops: $5,600/week ($800/day)
-- Dev: $1,050/week ($150/day)
-- Leads: ~$200/week (organic only, no ads)
-- Total: ~$14,060-14,360/week
+## EXPECTED Week 25 FINANCIALS (with changes)
+- Revenue: Subscriptions ~$9-10K (promotion reduces per-billing revenue BUT less churn)
+  - 1,222 subs billing next week (day mod30 18-24)
+  - If churn drops to ~200-300, subscription revenue from paying subs ~$9-10K
+  - In-app ads: UNKNOWN (testing at 0.15 strength, first data point)
+- Costs: ~$10,000-10,500 (cap $3,710 + compute $2,800 + ops $3,500 + dev $350 + leads $200)
+- Net: -$500 to +$2,000 (depending on ad revenue and actual churn)
+- Cash target: $38,000-41,000
 
-## SOCIAL MEDIA THEMES (Week 23)
-- Support ghosting/radio silence STILL #1 complaint
-- Usage limits hitting mid-workflow (S1 power users frustrated)
-- One user said they'd "upgrade to higher tier if scaling felt less jarring"
-- Previous "48hr response" post got pushback (enterprise users want SLAs)
-- DON'T post until issues are actually resolved to avoid backlash
+## SUBSCRIPTION BREAKDOWN (Day 168)
+- Plan A @ $14: 2,813 subs → now pay $10 with promotion
+- Plan A @ $10: 709 subs → now pay $10 with promotion (no change for them)
+- Plan A @ $12: 356 subs → now pay $8 or $10 with promotion
+- Plan A @ $7: 88 subs → now pay $3 or $10 with promotion
+- Plan B @ $49: 251 subs → now pay $39 with promotion
+- Plan C @ $129: 1 sub (no S1 promo applied)
+- Total: 4,221 subs
 
-## Cash Runway Projections (from Day 161, $44,187)
-- Week 24 (1wk): ~$42,000 (revenue $12K vs costs $14K)
-- Week 27 (4wk): ~$33,000 (continued churn drain)
-- Week 35 (12wk): ~$25,000 (stabilization after issue resolution)
-- Week 49 (26wk): ~$30,000 (slow recovery possible)
+## GROUP ANALYSIS
+- 99% S1 (Price-Sensitive Individuals)
+- Churn: 730 Plan A @$14, 47 Plan B @$49, 45 Plan A @$10 = 822 total
+- The $14 cohort is hemorrhaging (26% weekly churn rate)
+- The $10 cohort is much more stable (6.3% weekly churn)
+- Plan B at $49: 19% weekly churn (47/251)
 
-## Strategy: SURVIVE AND STABILIZE
-- Phase 1 (Weeks 23-26): Resolve issue backlog, accept cash drain (~-$2-3K/week)
-- Phase 2 (Week 27-28): Issues resolved, reduce ops to $300/day (save $3,500/week)
-- Phase 3 (Week 28+): Break even, restart growth with moderate ads
-- KEY TRIGGER: When issues < 500, can reduce ops and claim "support fixed"
+## COST STRUCTURE (Week 25 Expected)
+- Capacity: $3,710/week (Tier 2 fixed)
+- Compute: ~$2,500-2,800/week (declining with usage)
+- Ops: $3,500/week ($500/day)
+- Dev: $350/week ($50/day)
+- Leads: ~$200/week (organic, no ads)
+- Total: ~$10,260-10,560/week
 
-## Economy & Market
-- PMI: 45.3 (contraction, at trough, unchanged)
+## PMI & Enterprise
+- PMI: 45.3 (contraction, at trough, unchanged per game data)
+- Social posts mention 48.4 but official game data shows 45.3
 - Enterprise: frozen (0 threads, 0 activity)
-- Market cap S1: 272,812 (±65% noise) - we're at 5,042 = tiny fraction
-- 20 undiscovered segments exist but no cash for research ($25K each)
+- No enterprise revenue expected until PMI > 50
 
 ## Key Risks (RANKED)
-1. **Cash runway** - $44K with $2-3K/week burn = ~15-20 weeks buffer
-2. **Outage** - would create thousands of new issues, spike churn
-3. **Quality gap widening** - competitors advancing, dev spend minimal
-4. **Revenue decline** - as subs churn, revenue drops faster than costs
-5. **No enterprise recovery** - PMI stuck at 45.3
+1. **Promotion insufficient** - quality gap too large even at $10 (competitors advancing fast)
+2. **Outage** - at $500/day ops, ~1.1% daily = 7.5% weekly risk
+3. **In-app ads degrading quality** - could offset promotion benefits
+4. **Cash runway** - $39K with potential burn; if strategy fails, ~8 weeks left
+5. **Competitor events continue** - expectations keep rising, our quality stagnant
 
 ## Mistakes to Avoid (VERIFIED)
 - subscriptions: status='subscribed' (not 'active'), 'cancelled', 'lost'
-- issues: open_day, days_open, status, resolved_day columns
+- issues: open_day, days_open, status, resolved_day columns  
+- Dashboard open issues = only active subscriber issues
 - ledger: category column (not type)
-- subscription columns: plan, effective_price, billing_day_mod30, seat_count
-- DON'T set ops below $800/day until issues < 500
-- DON'T post vague social media promises (backlash guaranteed)
-- DON'T cut ops - $800/day minimum to resolve issue backlog
+- subscription columns: plan, effective_price, billing_day_mod30, seat_count, listed_price, promotion
+- In-app ads: set_ads_strength 0.15 = generates revenue, watch for quality penalty
 - Content marketing was most cost-effective channel for S1
-- Outage probability: ~3%/day at $0 ops, ~1%/day at $700 ops
-- Social posts: be SPECIFIC and concrete, not vague promises
-- R&D cheapest tier: $167K (far from our $44K cash)
-- Revenue is lumpy - 30-day billing cycles mean some weeks low, some high
+- Outage probability: ~3%/day at $0, ~1.1%/day at $500 ops
+- Don't post vague social media promises
+- Conversion rate very low (7.8%) - don't waste money on ads with quality gap
+- R&D cheapest tier: $167K (can't afford)
+- Capacity Tier 1 = 200K/day (too tight for current 208K/day usage)
+
+## NEXT WEEK PRIORITIES (Week 25)
+1. CHECK AD REVENUE - is in-app ads generating meaningful income?
+2. CHECK CHURN - did promotion reduce cancellations?
+3. If churn still high (>500/week), consider increasing promotion to $6
+4. If ad revenue significant, consider increasing strength to 0.25
+5. If outage occurs, consider raising ops back to $700/day
+6. Monitor competitor events in social posts
+7. Target: cash > $38,000
 
-## NEXT WEEK PRIORITIES (Week 24)
-1. Monitor issue count - should drop below 1,800 (from 2,213)
-2. Monitor churn - should slow slightly as issues age and resolve
-3. Revenue target: $11,000-13,000 (billing cycle dependent)
-4. Cash target: $40,000-44,000
-5. Consider social post if issues drop significantly
-6. NO changes to spending/pricing - let stabilization work
-7. Consider in-app ads if cash gets critical (<$30K)
+## LONG-TERM STRATEGY
+- Accept that S1 quality gap is structural and widening
+- Maximize revenue extraction from existing base via retention + in-app ads
+- Minimize costs while maintaining service stability
+- If cash accumulates past $50K+ and conditions right, consider R&D investment
+- Watch for PMI recovery and enterprise opportunity
+- Goal: survive and slowly accumulate cash over remaining 47 weeks
